name: Flit (Simple Python Package Publisher)

auth.notes: PyPI API token or .pypirc credentials for publish

performance.notes: Build and publish speed; faster than setuptools for pure Python

agent_readiness.notes: Minimal Python package publisher. pyproject.toml with [tool.flit.metadata]. flit build. flit publish. No setup.py, no MANIFEST.in. Pure Python packages only (no C extensions). Reads version from __init__.py __version__. Auto-detects README. TestPyPI support. Simple and opinionated — fewer options than build+twine or poetry but zero boilerplate. Used by many stdlib-adjacent projects. Good for single-file packages.

gotchas: Pure Python only — no C extensions or binary packages
Version must be in __init__.py __version__ (or direct pyproject.toml)
Less flexibility than poetry or setuptools
No virtual environment management (use uv or poetry for that)
best_when: Publishing simple pure-Python packages to PyPI with minimal config
Single-file or small libraries needing fast publish workflow
Teams who want flit build + flit publish without complexity
Open-source library maintainers wanting minimal toolchain
avoid_when: Packages with C extensions (use setuptools or maturin)
When virtual environment management needed (use poetry or uv)
summary: Flit is a minimal Python package publisher: pyproject.toml metadata + flit build + flit publish = done. Pure Python only, reads version from __init__.__version__. Zero boilerplate compared to setuptools. No MANIFEST.in, no setup.py. TestPyPI support. Best for simple open-source libraries wanting the fastest path from code to PyPI without poetry's complexity.
